items.title: Police seize guns and ammunition from home of 'white supremacist'

children.content: Police raid a home as part of an operation to reassess licensed gun holders in the wake of the Bondi Beach shooting.

items.title: Controversial $500m South West wind farm approved

image.altText: Ariel shot of the Scott River winding through green bushland

children.content: WA's Regional Development Assessment Panel approves Synergy's proposed 20-turbine wind farm in the state's South West, ending a period of approvals that has divided the community.

items.title: 'Unfathomable': Truckies want higher penalties for dangerous driving

children.content: Truck drivers say the fines for dangerous overtaking should match drink driving penalties due to the risk it poses to other road users

items.title: WA's rental vacancy half the national average, according to new report

children.content: Western Australians are increasingly being driven towards financial distress and homelessness as the cost of renting continues to surge, according to new data from Anglicare WA.

image.altText: Brett and his mother sit on the couch in a dimly lit, small apartment living room.

items.title: Rare high-end perfume ingredient ambergris washes up on WA beach

children.content: A highly prized, rare ingredient used in the production of luxury perfumes washes up on a Denmark beach — and it comes from the intestine of sperm whales.

gallery.altText: Bald man holding black and brown circular object size of a coconut

thumbnail.altText: Bald man holding black and brown circular object size of a coconut

items.title: Officer who laughed at prison death among six sanctioned by WA Police

children.content: Six WA Police officers have been sanctioned over their treatment of a man in their custody who later died in prison, after the ABC revealed a constable dismissed his threat of suicide, and laughed at news of his death.

items.title: 'Chaotic scenes': How the infamous Sirengate mess transpired 20 years ago today

children.content: Two decades ago, the Dockers and Saints met at York Park in Launceston. The siren sounded but the match continued — and the dreaded word "gate" was added to another footy scandal.

gallery.altText: Dockers players celebrate prematurely at York Park
A Fremantle AFL player holds his coach back as the St Kilda captain confrtons him and a Saints player points in the background.

items.title: Perth awakes blanketed in a thick shroud of fog — see the pictures here

children.content: Perth has been blanketed with thick fog, prompting warnings for motorists to use caution in the conditions.

items.title: Chaotic final minute sees Collingwood and Hawthorn settle for draw

children.content: A goal after the siren from Dylan Moore allows Hawthorn to claim a draw against Collingwood at the MCG. Follow live.

image.altText: Steele Sidebottom in action against Hawthorn

items.title: Rebel Wilson trial told of 'culturally inappropriate Indian outfit' post

children.content: Rebel Wilson tells a court several social media posts targeting the lead actor on her film The Deb are all true, on her final day of cross-examination in a defamation trial.

image.altText: Rebel Wilson outside court April 30 for defamation trial

items.title: Australia's summer saw the dawn of the big battery, as gas drops to 25-year low

children.content: A hot summer saw Australians use record amounts of electricity, but the growing share of renewable energy continued to push fossil fuels out of the grid, driving gas generation to its lowest level in 25 years.

image.altText: Silhouette of transmission lines at dusk with sun flaring through

items.title: Artist who taught himself to paint after seeing Roy and HG portrait wins prize

children.content: Sean Layh has won the $3,000 Packing Room Prize at this year's Archibald Prize for his portrait of Jacob Collins, titled The tragicall historie of Hamlet, Prince of Denmarke.

gallery.altText: Portrait of Jacob Collins by Sean Layh; a man laying in a disheveled bed.

thumbnail.altText: Portrait of Jacob Collins by Sean Layh; a man laying in a disheveled bed.

items.title: Royal commission recommends national firearms agreement and buyback scheme

children.content: The Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion releases its interim report, making 14 recommendations.

image.altText: Sam Mostyn holds a copy of the interim report.

items.title: Longest-serving WA MP, war hero Bill Grayden, dies at 105

children.content: WA's longest-serving parliamentarian, war hero Bill Grayden, is being remembered for his service to his country and community.

image.altText: Kokoda campaign veteran Bill Grayden at a service in Kings Park.

items.title: More women come forward after sepsis case linked to pedicure

children.content: More Perth women share their experience of needing surgery after having an adverse reaction to nail salon services, triggering a warning from the consumer watchdog.

items.title: Docker O'Driscoll reveals he contemplated suicide in emotional post

children.content: Fremantle Dockers player Nathan O'Driscoll reveals his mental health struggles in an emotional social media post, amid a push from players for the AFL to implement a mental health round.

items.title: Hanson billed taxpayers for flights to fundraisers and to catch Rinehart jet to Florida

children.content: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson billed taxpayers for two trips that included party fundraisers, discussions with disaffected National Barnaby Joyce, a Gina Rinehart event and return flights to connect the senator to her private jet to Florida, sponsored by her major donor, Mrs Rinehart.

image.altText: A woman in a checked shirt sits in the cockpit of a small plane, grinning.

items.title: Tim Picton's accused killer pleads not guilty to manslaughter

children.content: The man accused of killing former Labor identity Tim Picton outside a Northbridge venue has pleaded not guilty to his manslaughter.

items.title: From cereal to clothing, see how the price of everyday items has changed

children.content: If it feels like everything is getting more expensive, that's because it is. With the rate of inflation rising this week, we've tracked how the price of everyday items has increased.

items.title: Shock closure of 12 WA childcare centres

items.title: WA farmers seeding their 'riskiest crop in years' welcome early rain

children.content: Widespread rain delivers timely relief to grain growers across the southern Wheatbelt, as they balance seeding decisions against soaring diesel and fertiliser costs.

items.title: New course helps teens navigate unique outback driving hazards

children.content: A new defensive driving course being offered in Kalgoorlie aims to equip young motorists with the skills to cope with the unique challenges of outback driving.

image.altText: A teenage boy behind the wheel of a car next to an instructor in a driver trainer course.

items.title: Monitor appointed to address tensions between councillors at Perth shire

children.content: A fifth local authority in WA has been assigned an independent monitor, this time at the council's request, to focus on council member conduct and improving relationships.

items.title: Fuel shock pushes civil contractors to cut jobs and pause works
